content.problemStatement:
There is a programming contest held by ShareChat. According to the contest rules, submissions do not receive any feedback until the end of the contest. The judges wanted to benefit from this fact to make their favourite contestants win the contest.

In one of the problems, there were a total of NNN submissions (numbered 111 through NNN) received. The judges prepared a total of MMM tests (numbered 111 through MMM) for this problem. For each submission iii and each test jjj, we know that the time this submission ran on this test is Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j‚Äã and whether it produced a correct answer or not.

The judges can still decide the time limit for this problem. For each submission, they have decided whether they wanted it to pass all the tests or not. You are given an integer sequence D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DND_1, D_2, \dots, D_ND1‚Äã,D2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,DN‚Äã; Di=1D_i = 1Di‚Äã=1 denotes that submission iii should pass all tests and Di=0D_i = 0Di‚Äã=0 denotes that it should fail on at least one test, either by exceeding the time limit (getting TLE) or producing an incorrect answer (getting WA).

Now, the judges want to decide the time limit of the problem and the subset of tests they should use so that each submission gets the desired verdict (passes or fails).
constraints: the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
the sum of MMM for all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
1‚â§Ai,j‚â§1081 \le A_{i, j} \le 10^81‚â§Ai,j‚Äã‚â§108 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DND_1, D_2, \dots, D_ND1‚Äã,D2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,DN‚Äã.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains MMM space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,MA_{i, 1}, A_{i, 2}, \dots, A_{i, M}Ai,1‚Äã,Ai,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai,M‚Äã.
NNN more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains MMM space-separated integers Bi,1,Bi,2,‚Ä¶,Bi,MB_{i, 1}, B_{i, 2}, \dots, B_{i, M}Bi,1‚Äã,Bi,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Bi,M‚Äã. Here, Bi,j=1B_{i, j} = 1Bi,j‚Äã=1 denotes that submission iii produced a correct answer on test jjj and Bi,j=0B_{i, j} = 0Bi,j‚Äã=0 denotes that it did not.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing the string `"NO"` if it is impossible to find a solution or `"YES"` otherwise. If a solution exists, print two more lines. The first of these lines should contain two space-separated integers TTT (the time limit, 1‚â§T‚â§1091 \le T \le 10^91‚â§T‚â§109) and KKK (the number of tests chosen). The last line should contain KKK space-separated integers denoting the indices of the chosen tests.
